mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month acceleration in the unemployment rate signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or null 60-day forward return in > 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when tested against a baseline single-proxy model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing and utility output that directly boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The positive return signal reverses to negative when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, or the hypothesis fails to maintain a positive Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 320.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market expansion that supports aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth coupled with rising personal consumption expenditures signals broad-based economic expansion that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of accommodative monetary policy.
failure_condition: The positive return signal fails to materialize when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the hypothesis yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window in low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in the industrial production index signals an immediate supply-side shock that disrupts corporate output and triggers risk-off selling, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a > 0.5% monthly INDPRO decline, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.
